title: Post-operative wound review

follow_up.text: Supports clinician-documented postoperative wound-care information, infection-warning discussion, and who to contact if concern develops.
follow_up.rationale: Supports postoperative wound-care and infection-warning documentation for postoperative wound review; no decision is generated.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-documented postoperative wound-care information, infection-warning discussion, and who to contact if concern develops.

source.title: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.4 — information, wound care, and infection recognition

follow_up.text: Defines the postoperative surgical-wound population and supports wound dressing, cleansing, wound-healing, and structured postoperative wound-care documentation.
follow_up.rationale: Supports dressing, cleansing, healing and structured wound review for postoperative wound review; no decision is generated.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Defines the postoperative surgical-wound population and supports wound dressing, cleansing, wound-healing, and structured postoperative wound-care documentation.

follow_up.text: Supports review of suspected infection, cellulitis, microbiology context, structured wound care, and local heat, redness, pain, swelling, discharge, separation, abscess, fever, or systemic signs.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Operation, wound dimensions, closure material, drain, tissue viability, collection, microbiology, treatment, staple or suture removal, escalation and follow-up remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for postoperative wound review.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
